content.problemStatement:
The followers of Psycho-Helmet religion follow a peculiar calendar – a normal year contains NNN days. Every KKK-th year is a “MOB” year. For example, if K=4K = 4K=4, then years 4,8,12,16…4, 8, 12, 16 \ldots4,8,12,16… are “MOB” years. A “MOB” year contains MMM additional days i.e. it contains N+MN+MN+M days.

Given XXX, determine whether the day after (X−1)(X-1)(X−1) days from Day 111 of Year 111 falls in a “MOB” year.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing NNN, MMM, KKK and XXX — the number of days in a normal year, the number of days in a “MOB” year, the number of years between two “MOB” years and the integer XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` on a new line if the day after (X−1)(X-1)(X−1) days from Year 111, Day 111 falls in a “MOB” year, and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers N, M, K and X.
- N: number of days in a normal year.
- M: additional days in a MOB year (so a MOB year has N+M days).
- K: every K‑th year is a MOB year (years K, 2K, 3K, …).
- X: we consider the X‑th day starting from Day 1 of Year 1.
For each test case we have to decide whether that day lies inside a MOB year.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the X‑th day falls in a MOB year, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we compute blockDays = K*N+M and compare the remainder of X modulo blockDays with (K-1)*N.
- Case 1: blockDays=9, X%9=8, (K-1)*N=6 → 8>6 → YES.
- Case 2: blockDays=22, X%22=6, (K-1)*N=12 → 6≤12 → NO.
- Case 3: blockDays=22, X%22=17, 17>12 → YES.
K=1 means every year is a MOB year. Hence any X (including 7) falls inside a MOB year.
blockDays = 6*4+5 = 29. X%29 = 20. (K-1)*N = 5*4 = 20. Since remainder is not greater than normalDays and not zero, the day lies in a normal year → NO.

solution.approach:
A block of K consecutive years consists of K‑1 normal years and one MOB year. The total number of days in such a block is:
    blockDays = K * N + M.
If X is a multiple of blockDays, the X‑th day is the last day of a block, which belongs to the MOB year at the end of the block.
Otherwise let rem = X % blockDays (1 ≤ rem < blockDays). The first (K‑1) * N days of the block belong to normal years, the remaining days belong to the MOB year. Therefore the answer is YES iff rem == 0 or rem > (K‑1) * N.
The computation uses only a few integer operations, O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.
